MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JUNE 22, 2010. NO APPEARANCE BY EITHER PARTY. (2) PLAINTIFF AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED AS THE DOCUMENTS SHALL BE PRODUCED IN THE MANNER SET FORTH IN THE RESPONSES, SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE COSTS OF PRODUCING THE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ARE NOT THE RESPONSIBILITY OF PLAINTIFF. SEE SAN DIEGO UNIFIED PORT DISTRICT V DOUGLAS E. BARNHARDT, INC., (2002) 95 CAL.APP.4TH 1400, 1405: 'PARTIES ARE EXPECTED TO BEAR EXPENSES TYPICALLY INVOLVED IN RESPONDING TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS, SUCH AS THE EXPENSE OF PRODUCING DOCUMENTS, ANSWERING INTERROGATORIES OR SUBMITTING TO DEPOSITIONS.' THERE ARE NO SIGNIFICANT 'SPECIAL ATTENDANT' COSTS BEYOND THOSE TYPICALLY INVOLVED IN RESPONDING TO ROUTINE DISCOVERY. PRODUCTION SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT HEWLETT JR., NOT REPORTED (612).

MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JUNE 22, 2010. NO APPEARANCE BY EITHER PARTY. (3) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED AS TO NO. 2. DEFENDANT SHALL RESPOND FURTHER, IDENTIFYING THE DUTIES OF THE PERSONS IN EACH CATEGORY OF JOB AND THE NUMBERS OF PERSONS IN EACH CATEGORY. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 5, 6, AND 8 AS THE RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE. THE MOTION IS MOOT AS TO NOS. 4, 7, 10 AND 11 AS DEFENDANT HAS AGREED TO SUPPLEMENT THOSE RESPONSES. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 1, 3, AND 13 AS THE RESPONSES MAY BE MADE BY MAKING THE DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE FOR INSPECTION AS THEY ARE MAINTAINED IN THE REGULAR COURSE OF BUSINESS, SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER. FURTHER RESPONSES AND INSPECTION SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT HEWLETT JR., NOT REPORTED (612).

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2010 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-04-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Responses To Demand For Production; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 22, 2010. No appearance by either party. (1) Plaintiff AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Compliance With Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents is granted. Documents shall be produced subject to a protective order where requested, limiting disclosure to anyone not involved in the litigation and requiring the information to be held in confidence. Compliance shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett Hewlett Jr., not reported (612).

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant King Security Services, Inc. To Demands For Production And All Responsive Documents; Request For Sanction
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 22, 2010. No appearance by either party. (2) Plaintiff AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents And All Responsive Documents is granted as the documents shall be produced in the manner set forth in the responses, subject to a protective order. The costs of producing the responsive documents are not the responsibility of Plaintiff. See San Diego Unified Port District v Douglas E. Barnhardt, Inc., (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 1400, 1405: 'parties are expected to bear expenses typically involved in responding to discovery requests, such as the expense of producing documents, answering interrogatories or submitting to depositions.' There are no significant 'special attendant' costs beyond those typically involved in responding to routine discovery.
Production shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett Hewlett Jr., not reported (612).

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 22, 2010. No appearance by either party. (3) Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted as to No. 2. Defendant shall respond further, identifying the duties of the persons in each category of job and the numbers of persons in each category.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 5, 6, and 8 as the responses are inadequate. The motion is moot as to Nos. 4, 7, 10 and 11 as Defendant has agreed to supplement those responses.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 1, 3, and 13 as the responses may be made by making the documents available for inspection as they are maintained in the regular course of business, subject to a protective order.
Further responses and inspection shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett Hewlett Jr., not reported (612).

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Demands For The Production Of Documents, Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Michael I Weiss, appearing telephonically for Plailntiff and moving party: American Home Assurance Co.
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE COMPANY, A QUALIFIED NEW's Motion To Compel Answers To Demands For The Production Of Documents is granted. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $302.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
